ieee_en:
# [Paper Title]

**[Author 1]**$^1$, **[Author 2]**$^2$
$^1$[Department, University, City, Country] $^2$[Department, University]

:::abstract
[Context: 1-2 sentences establishing the problem domain.]
[Gap: What is missing or insufficient in current approaches.]
[Method: Your approach in one sentence.]
[Result: Key quantitative finding, e.g., "improves X by Y%".]
[Implication: Why this matters.]
:::

**Index Terms:** [term 1], [term 2], [term 3], [term 4]

---

## 1. Introduction

<!-- Structure: Problem -> Why it matters -> What's been tried -> Gap -> Your contribution -->

**Contributions.** This paper makes the following contributions:
- C1:
- C2:
- C3:

**Organization.** The remainder of this paper is organized as follows. Section 2 provides background. Section 3 describes ... Section 4 presents ... Section 5 evaluates ... Section 6 discusses limitations. Section 7 concludes.

---

## 2. Background and Related Work

### 2.1 [Topic Area]
### 2.2 [Related Approaches]
### 2.3 [Gap in Existing Work]

---

## 3. Threat Model

### 3.1 Assets
### 3.2 Adversary Capabilities
### 3.3 Trust Boundaries
### 3.4 Assumptions and Scope

---

## 4. Proposed Approach

### 4.1 Overview
### 4.2 Design Details

<!-- Example equation: -->
$$
\\mathcal{L} = \\sum_{i=1}^{N} \\ell(f(x_i), y_i) + \\lambda \\|\\theta\\|_2^2
$$

:::algorithm [Procedure Name]
Input: [input description]
Output: [output description]
1. [Step]
2. [Step]
3. Return [result]
:::

---

## 5. Evaluation

### 5.1 Experimental Setup

*Table 1. Experimental environment.*
| Component | Specification |
|-----------|---------------|
| Hardware | |
| Software | |
| Dataset | |

### 5.2 Metrics
### 5.3 Baselines
### 5.4 Results

*Table 2. Main results (mean +/- std over 5 runs).*
| Method | Metric A | Metric B | Metric C |
|--------|----------|----------|----------|
| Baseline 1 | | | |
| Proposed | | | |

### 5.5 Ablation Study

---

## 6. Discussion

### 6.1 Limitations
### 6.2 Broader Impact

---

## 7. Conclusion

---

## Acknowledgments

---

## References

<!-- IEEE style: [1] A. Author and B. Author, "Title," in Proc. Conf., Year, pp. xx-xx. -->
`,

## NeurIPS Paper Checklist

<!-- Main paper: 9 pages max. Checklist is mandatory but excluded from page limit. -->
- [ ] Claims: Do main claims match theory/experiments?
- [ ] Limitations: Are limitations discussed?
- [ ] Theory: Are all assumptions stated?
- [ ] Experiments: Code/data availability?
- [ ] Reproducibility: Seeds, hyperparameters, error bars?
- [ ] Ethics: IRB, consent, privacy?
